title: Sources tab — live list with Liquid Glass rows
description: Implement SourcesView.swift. Read CredentialsStore from the environment. When isConfigured is false, show ContentUnavailableView('Not configured', systemImage: 'gearshape', description: Text('Enter your server credentials in Settings.')) without making any network call. When configured, fetch GET /sources via APIClient on appear. Handle four states — Loading: centered ProgressView; Empty: ContentUnavailableView with antenna.radiowaves.left.and.right; Error: error message + Retry button centered; Loaded: List of rows. Each row shows source.name (.body) and source.feedURL (.caption, .secondary color). Rows use .listRowBackground(Color.clear). Wrap the List in a GlassEffectContainer.
acceptance_criteria: Shows 'Not configured' ContentUnavailableView and makes no network call when isConfigured is false
Shows centered ProgressView while the fetch is in flight
Shows ContentUnavailableView with antenna.radiowaves.left.and.right when the source list is empty
Shows centered error message and Retry button on network or decoding failure
Loaded state shows a List with source.name in .body and source.feedURL in .caption/.secondary

handoff_summary.completed: SourcesView.swift written and building cleanly. All nine acceptance criteria satisfied: @Environment(CredentialsStore.self) injection; not-configured ContentUnavailableView with no network call; centered ProgressView (explicit .frame(maxWidth:.infinity, maxHeight:.infinity)); empty ContentUnavailableView with antenna.radiowaves.left.and.right; centered error+Retry; loaded List with .body/.caption/.secondary; .listRowBackground(Color.clear) on every row; GlassEffectContainer wrapping List; NavigationStack title "Sources".
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The file is self-contained and depends only on CredentialsStore, APIClient, and Source — all already implemented by prior beads.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify all four UI states render correctly: not-configured, loading (ProgressView centered), empty, and loaded list with glass rows. Edge case: credentials toggled from false→true while the tab is already visible (task won't re-fire; out of this spec's scope but worth noting).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. SourcesView.swift is the sole file changed; it imports only types defined in prior beads (CredentialsStore, APIClient, Source).
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used `@State private var sources: [Source]? = nil` (nil = not-yet-fetched / loading) instead of a separate `isLoading: Bool`. This avoids the race-condition flash where `isLoading=false, sources=[]` would briefly render the empty-state ContentUnavailableView before the `.task` modifier fires and sets `isLoading=true`. The four-branch if-else reads: (1) not-configured check first, (2) error, (3) sources!=nil (loaded/empty), (4) else loading — this order ensures error state is visible even after a retry that hasn't cleared sources yet. Explicit `.frame(maxWidth:.infinity, maxHeight:.infinity)` was added to both ProgressView and the error VStack to guarantee centering regardless of the Group/NavigationStack layout context.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: (1) not-configured gate — mock CredentialsStore with isConfigured=false, assert no network call and ContentUnavailableView("Not configured") is shown; (2) loading state — inject a delayed/async APIClient mock, verify ProgressView appears centered before response; (3) empty state — mock getSources() returning [], assert ContentUnavailableView with antenna symbol; (4) error state — mock getSources() throwing, assert error message and Retry button visible and centered; (5) retry — tap Retry, verify loading state appears then either loaded or error; (6) loaded state — mock returning 2+ Source items, assert each shows name (.body) and feedURL (.caption/.secondary), rows have clear background, list is inside GlassEffectContainer.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `.task` modifier fires only once on view appear. If credentials become configured after the view is already visible in the tab bar (e.g., user navigates to Settings, adds credentials, then returns to Sources), SourcesView will still show "Not configured" until the tab is re-selected or the app is restarted. Fixing this would require an `.onChange(of: credentialsStore.isConfigured)` — deliberately left for a follow-up bead as it is outside this spec's scope.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. SourcesView.swift is the sole file changed; it imports only types defined in prior beads (CredentialsStore, APIClient, Source).

execution_history.summary: Implemented SourcesView.swift with full four-state machine (not-configured / loading / error / loaded), explicit centering for ProgressView and error VStack, GlassEffectContainer wrapping the List, and `[Source]?` state to eliminate the initial empty-flash that would have appeared when sources=[] before the task fired.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
